decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.28°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. The most effective primary lever is to increase the heat spreader conductivity to improve lateral heat distribution away from the chip center. I will increase the spreader conductivity by a factor of 1.5 (from 135.0 to 202.5 W/(m*K)), which is within the allowed [0.5x, 2.0x] step ratio and bounds.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity enhances lateral heat spreading, reducing the thermal gradient and lowering the peak temperature at the chip center.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards or below 85.0°C
More uniform temperature distribution across the heat spreader
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate conductivity or boundary conditions rather than lateral spreading.
High conductivity materials may be cost-prohibitive in real-world applications, though this is a simulation study.
